text.title: Decomposition: Django Monolith to Bounded Apps Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Decomp creates a `utils` or `common` app that every other app imports.
antipattern.root-cause: Decomposed along technical layers, not ownership boundaries.
antipattern.fix: Delete the utils app; push helpers into the owning app or into contracts_shared with strict scope.

antipattern.symptom: Cross-app code reaches `from orders.models import Order` instead of `from orders.contracts import get_order`.
antipattern.root-cause: explicit-app-contract rule not enforced; no importlinter contract.
antipattern.fix: Add importlinter contract forbidding non-contracts cross-app imports; refactor leak sites.

antipattern.symptom: Model moved in PR-A; FK rewrites in PR-B two weeks later; ContentType broken between.
antipattern.fix: Re-do as one PR; revert split if needed.
Move the task to orders or call orders' contracts function from the payments task.
